meta.title: China Industry Atlas
meta.subtitle: One City, One Industry
meta.tagline: A field map of China's manufacturing clusters — city by city, market by market.
meta.methodology: This atlas maps China's industrial clustering — the 'one city, one industry' pattern — at city and town level. The durable value is the specialization map: which place owns which category, and which market or district inside it. Population and GDP figures are approximate reference points drawn from recent public data and should be treated as orders of magnitude, not precise current values; cluster facts and named anchor firms are the stable signal. Verify supplier-specific claims, certifications, and current export-control or import rules independently before committing.

regions.name: Pearl River Delta
regions.blurb: China's original manufacturing powerhouse and still the most diverse. Centered on Shenzhen, Guangzhou, and Dongguan, it runs from electronics and toys to furniture, jewelry, and advanced tech. Town-level specialization is the defining feature.

regions.name: Yangtze River Delta
regions.blurb: Advanced manufacturing married to the world's best logistics. Shanghai is the finance and shipping core; the surrounding cities each own a category, from semiconductors and home textiles to molds, fabric, and small commodities.

regions.name: Bohai Rim
regions.blurb: Heavy industry, home appliances, and a string of surprising single-product clusters — false eyelashes, fishing tackle, kites, hair products, costumes — alongside aerospace and petrochemicals.

regions.name: West Coast of the Taiwan Strait
regions.blurb: Sports goods, new energy, and cross-strait industrial cooperation. Quanzhou and Jinjiang own athletic footwear; Ningde owns batteries; Xiamen leads third-generation semiconductors.

regions.name: Central & Western China
regions.provinces: Anhui · Hubei · Hunan · Henan · Shaanxi · Sichuan · Chongqing · Inner Mongolia
regions.blurb: The fastest-growing growth poles, leveraging lower costs, strong state support, and China-Europe rail. Hefei, Wuhan, and Xi'an lead in chips, displays, NEV, optics, and solar; the region also holds rare earths, rail, and construction machinery.

cities.name: Shenzhen

stats.note: Largest single-city electronics ecosystem on earth

cities.summary: If it has a circuit board, it was probably designed, prototyped, or sourced through Shenzhen. Forty years from fishing village to the densest electronics supply chain in the world. The advantage is not any single factory but proximity: components, board houses, mold shops, assembly, and the people who know how to connect them all sit within a one-hour radius.

markets.name: Huaqiangbei (HQB)
markets.note: Not one mall but a district. SEG Electronics Market and Huaqiang Electronics World are the anchors; Mingtong Digital City is the IC/component floors (chips, passives, connectors); the old SEG Communications side trades phones, parts, and repair gear. Component floors are where you see parts 6-12 months before they reach Western catalogs.

markets.name: Shuibei
markets.note: One of China's largest gold and fine-jewelry trading clusters; manufacturing, casting, and wholesale in one district.

markets.name: Bao'an & Longhua
markets.note: Where the actual contract manufacturing and box-build lives once you leave the trading floors. DJI, Foxconn campuses, countless mid-size assembly houses.

markets.name: Guangming / Pingshan
markets.note: Battery, BMS, and newer hard-tech tenants pushed out from the urban core for space.

markets.name: Dafen
markets.note: A genuine 'one product' enclave — a large share of the world's hand-painted reproductions.

cities.sourcing: Treat HQB as intelligence, not just procurement. The trading floors reveal what is becoming cheap and what is becoming possible; the real production sits out in Bao'an and Longhua. For small runs you can buy components in the morning and have boards back in days, which is the genuine Shenzhen edge. Verify that a 'factory' is not just a trading desk with a borrowed showroom.

cities.name: Guangzhou
cities.summary: China's gateway to the world for over a thousand years and still the trading heart of the south. Guangzhou's strength is breadth and the wholesale-market model: enormous specialized markets where you can compare hundreds of suppliers in a single building, then drive to the factories that feed them in the surrounding towns.

markets.name: Zhongda Fabric Market
markets.focus: Largest fabric & trim cluster in the south
markets.note: Hundreds of buildings of textiles, linings, and accessories around the old Sun Yat-sen University textile district. The default place to source fabric in China.

markets.name: Shahe
markets.note: High-volume, low-price ready-made clothing; the engine behind a lot of cross-border apparel.

markets.name: Shisanhang / Baima
markets.note: Baima is mid-market; Shisanhang is ultra-fast cheap fashion near the railway station.

markets.name: Huadu - Shiling
markets.note: Shiling town makes a very large share of China's bags and leather accessories.

markets.name: Panyu
markets.note: A historic manufacturing base for Hong Kong jewelry brands; gold, gem-setting, OEM.

markets.name: Baiyun - cosmetics belt
markets.note: Large cluster of cosmetics, skincare, and daily-chemical contract manufacturers.

cities.sourcing: The wholesale markets are for comparison and small buys; for OEM you want the factories in Huadu, Panyu, and Baiyun that supply them. The Canton Fair (April & October, Pazhou) is overwhelming — walk and collect on day one, shortlist on day two, negotiate on day three. Never commit on the first pass.

cities.name: Dongguan
cities.summary: The classic 'world's factory' — but the real map is at town level. Dongguan is a constellation of specialized towns, each one a near-complete supply chain for a single category. Knowing which town makes your product is worth more than any single supplier name.

markets.name: Houjie
markets.note: Mid-to-high-end shoe OEM and the machinery/last/material supply chain that supports it.

markets.name: Dalang
markets.note: One of the world's largest knitwear towns; full chain from yarn to finished sweater, heavily automated.

markets.name: Humen
markets.note: Huge apparel cluster with its own wholesale markets; quick-turn fashion.

markets.name: Chang'an
markets.note: 5-metal hardware, precision molds, CNC — a backbone for the region's electronics and appliance makers.

markets.name: Qingxi / Changping / Shijie
markets.note: Mid-size assembly and components feeding the broader PRD electronics chain.

cities.sourcing: Ask which town first, then which factory. MOQs run higher than in trading-market cities because these are real production houses used to brand programs. Many now carry ESG/social-audit certifications, which matters for European buyers.

cities.name: Foshan
cities.summary: Centuries of furniture and ceramics, now China's largest furniture base and a powerhouse in home appliances, ceramic tile, and aluminum. Two districts carry global weight: Lecong for furniture and Shunde for appliances.

markets.name: Lecong Furniture Market
markets.focus: Largest furniture wholesale market in Asia
markets.note: Kilometres of showrooms (Sunlink, Louvre, etc.). The showrooms are the shop window — the better prices and customization are at the factories in Longjiang and surrounding towns.

markets.name: Shunde
markets.note: Home of Midea and Galanz; a near-complete appliance supply chain (motors, compressors, plastics, controls). Longjiang within Shunde is also a major furniture town.

markets.name: Nanzhuang
markets.note: A core of China's tile industry; Foshan tile is a global category leader.

markets.name: Dali / Lishui
markets.note: Aluminum extrusion and stainless processing clusters.

cities.sourcing: For furniture, get past the Lecong showrooms to the Longjiang factories. For appliances, Shunde gives you a complete component chain in one district, which de-risks both cost and lead time. White-glove export and installation services are now common for high-end furniture.

cities.name: Zhongshan
cities.summary: A cluster of single-product towns west of the Pearl River. Guzhen alone is the global center of decorative and LED lighting; other towns specialize in hardware, locks, casual wear, small appliances, and mahogany furniture.

markets.name: Guzhen
markets.note: The dominant global cluster for decorative, commercial, and LED lighting; thousands of makers and the Lighting Expo. Sourcing lighting elsewhere rarely makes sense.

markets.name: Xiaolan
markets.note: Major lock and hardware base.

markets.name: Shaxi
markets.note: Leisure and casual apparel town.

markets.name: Dachong
markets.note: Specialist hardwood furniture cluster.

cities.sourcing: For any lighting program, start in Guzhen — the density of competition and the complete chain (drivers, housings, optics, fixtures) is unmatched. Quality tiers are wide; check certifications (CE/UKCA, photometric files) carefully for the European market.

cities.name: Zhuhai
cities.summary: A quieter PRD city with two outsized industries: it is the world center for printer consumables (compatible ink and toner cartridges) and home to appliance giant Gree.

markets.name: Printer-consumables cluster
markets.note: A very large share of the world's aftermarket cartridges and refills ships from Zhuhai (Apex, Print-Rite ecosystem).

markets.name: Gree industrial base
markets.note: One of the world's largest residential AC makers.

cities.sourcing: For any printing-consumables or imaging-supplies program, Zhuhai is effectively the only address worth a sourcing trip.

cities.name: Shantou
cities.summary: The Chaoshan trading culture made Shantou a cluster of tightly held family supply chains. Chenghai district is one of the world's true toy capitals; Gurao is a global underwear town; the city is also a printing and packaging center.

markets.name: Chenghai
markets.note: A genuine global toy hub — a very large share of plastic toys, RC, and educational toys. Home of Auldey/Alpha. Complete chain from molds to packaging.

markets.name: Gurao
markets.note: One of the largest bra and underwear clusters anywhere; whole-town specialization.

markets.name: Shantou printing belt
markets.note: Strong printing, packaging, and trading-card production base.

cities.sourcing: Chaoshan suppliers are relationship-first and family-run; trust and repeat business move prices more than hard negotiation. For toys, the Chenghai chain (mold, injection, deco, packaging) sits in one district, which is a real lead-time advantage.

cities.name: Chaozhou
cities.summary: Chaozhou is one of China's great ceramics centers — daily-use porcelain, sanitaryware (toilets, basins), and ceramic crafts — plus a notable wedding-dress and embroidery cluster.

markets.name: Fengxi
markets.note: The core ceramics district; tableware, decorative, and craft porcelain.

markets.name: Sanitaryware cluster
markets.note: Major base for toilets, basins, and bathroom fixtures.

cities.sourcing: For ceramic tableware and sanitaryware, Chaozhou and neighboring Chaoshan towns offer deep specialization at competitive cost; freight planning matters because the product is heavy and fragile.

cities.name: Jieyang
cities.summary: Part of the Chaoshan cluster. Jieyang is a stainless-steel hardware and kitchenware base, while its county-city Puning is one of China's largest textile and underwear wholesale markets.

markets.name: Puning
markets.note: Massive garment and underwear trading market plus surrounding production.

markets.name: Stainless-steel cluster
markets.note: Stainless cookware, sinks, and hardware base.

cities.sourcing: Useful pairing with Shantou and Chaozhou on a single Chaoshan trip; the region rewards visiting because so much trade still runs on personal relationships.

cities.name: Huizhou
cities.summary: An electronics and new-energy extension of the eastern PRD: home of TCL and a strong lithium-battery and components base (Desay, EVE nearby).

markets.name: Zhongkai hi-tech zone
markets.note: Display, module, and component manufacturing.

markets.name: Battery & BMS cluster
markets.note: Cells, packs, and battery-management electronics (Desay-BYD/EVE ecosystem).

cities.sourcing: A good option when you want PRD electronics capability with a bit more space and lower cost than central Shenzhen, especially for battery-adjacent products.

cities.name: Jiangmen
cities.summary: A western PRD hardware base — stainless kitchenware, faucets and sanitary hardware, and motorcycle components, with growing battery-material and equipment tenants.

markets.name: Pengjiang hardware cluster
markets.note: Stainless kitchenware and metal-goods base.

cities.sourcing: A quieter, cost-effective alternative to Foshan/Zhongshan for metal hardware and kitchenware programs.

cities.name: Shanghai
cities.summary: The financial and logistics command center of the YRD, and increasingly a high-end manufacturing city in its own right: automotive (including Tesla's Gigafactory), semiconductors (SMIC, Hua Hong in Zhangjiang), biotech, and precision equipment.

markets.name: Zhangjiang Science City
markets.note: China's leading IC design and foundry cluster (SMIC, Hua Hong) plus a dense biotech/pharma corridor.

markets.name: Lingang / Gigafactory
markets.note: Tesla's Shanghai plant anchors an EV and equipment cluster.

markets.name: Hongqiao trade hub
markets.note: CIIE and major B2B trade infrastructure.

cities.sourcing: Shanghai is where you set up entities, finance, QC headquarters, and high-value or regulated production. For commodity goods it is expensive; for chips, pharma, autos, and precision equipment it is a first-tier address.

cities.name: Hangzhou
cities.summary: Where China's e-commerce and live-stream economy was born. Home of Alibaba and Ant, a hub for cross-border trade and digital commerce, and a historic silk and women's-fashion center.

markets.name: Sijiqing
markets.note: One of China's largest women's apparel wholesale markets.

markets.name: China Silk City
markets.note: Historic silk trading and production base.

markets.name: Cross-border e-commerce parks
markets.note: Where you set up Tmall/Douyin/cross-border selling and live-stream operations.

cities.sourcing: Come to Hangzhou to build a China sales channel, not primarily to source hardware. If you want to sell into China or run live-stream campaigns, this is the place to understand the playbook.

cities.name: Ningbo
cities.summary: A massive port married to a deep manufacturing base: auto parts, plastic molds, small appliances, and stationery. The port advantage (Ningbo-Zhoushan) means lower freight and faster transit, especially for heavy or bulky goods and vehicles.

markets.name: Cixi
markets.note: One of China's densest small-appliance clusters (heaters, fans, kitchen gadgets) plus plastics and connectors.

markets.name: Yuyao
markets.note: Yuyao Plastic City is a national plastics-trading and mold hub.

markets.name: Fenghua / stationery belt
markets.note: Strong writing-instrument and stationery base in the wider Ningbo area.

cities.sourcing: If the product is heavy, bulky, or vehicle-related, sourcing near Ningbo can save meaningfully on freight versus inland cities, and the city is the leading port for NEV exports via roll-on/roll-off.

cities.name: Yiwu
cities.summary: A small city with the world's largest small-commodities wholesale market. If you need variety in modest quantities — gifts, accessories, toys, holiday goods, daily necessities — Yiwu is the default. The cross-border e-commerce ecosystem here is mature and buyer-friendly.

markets.name: Yiwu International Trade City
markets.note: Five connected districts, 75,000+ booths, millions of SKUs. Districts are organized by category, so plan your route by product.

markets.name: Cross-border e-commerce parks
markets.note: Inspection, consolidation, and shipping for small international buyers under one roof.

cities.sourcing: Ideal for variety stores, gift, and FBA sellers; MOQs can be as low as a carton or two. Quality varies enormously booth to booth — always inspect samples and confirm the booth is a maker or a stable trader, not a one-season reseller.

cities.name: Wenzhou
cities.summary: Famous for entrepreneurial drive and a stack of single-product clusters: footwear, the world's lighters, eyeglasses, and — critically for hardware buyers — China's low-voltage electrical apparatus capital in Liushi/Yueqing.

markets.name: Liushi (Yueqing)
markets.note: The national center for breakers, contactors, relays, terminal blocks, and industrial controls. Home turf of Chint and Delixi. The first place to source electrical components.

markets.name: Lucheng footwear
markets.note: Broad shoe base spanning ultra-cheap to premium private label.

markets.name: Qiaotou (Yongjia)
markets.note: The 'button capital' — an extraordinary concentration of buttons and trims.

markets.name: Oubei valves
markets.note: Industrial valve and pump cluster.

cities.sourcing: For any product with electrical innards, Liushi/Yueqing is a strategic stop — components and finished controls in one place. For shoes, identify the quality tier first (ask for their European client list).

cities.name: Shaoxing
cities.summary: Home of China Textile City in Keqiao — the largest fabric distribution market in the world — plus a vast printing-and-dyeing base and the necktie capital of Shengzhou.

markets.name: Keqiao - China Textile City
markets.note: If you cannot find a fabric in Guangzhou's Zhongda, you will find it here. Enormous distribution plus a regional dyeing/finishing chain.

markets.name: Shengzhou
markets.note: Produces the large majority of China's neckties and a big share of the world's.

cities.sourcing: For fabric sourcing at scale, Keqiao and Guangzhou's Zhongda are the two poles. Confirm dyeing and finishing capability and environmental compliance — printing-and-dyeing is the part of the chain that draws the most scrutiny.

cities.name: Zhuji
cities.summary: Two world-leading single-product towns: Datang makes a staggering share of the world's socks, and Shanxiahu is the global center of freshwater-pearl farming and trading.

markets.name: Datang
markets.note: A whole-town sock economy — billions of pairs a year, complete chain from yarn to packaging.

markets.name: Shanxiahu
markets.note: The dominant global hub for freshwater pearls: farming, processing, grading, and wholesale.

cities.sourcing: For socks or freshwater pearls, this is effectively the source. For pearls, learn grading before you buy — the price range across grades is enormous.

cities.name: Taizhou (Zhejiang)
cities.summary: A manufacturing powerhouse: Huangyan is China's mold capital, Wenling and Yuhuan make pumps and motorcycle/auto parts, and the city is the birthplace of Geely. Strong in sewing machines and medical/pharma too.

markets.name: Huangyan
markets.note: The national center for plastic injection molds — the tooling that the rest of China's plastics industry depends on.

markets.name: Wenling / Yuhuan
markets.note: Dense clusters of pumps, motors, and vehicle components.

cities.sourcing: If you need tooling, Huangyan is where the best mold shops are; pairing a Taizhou mold maker with a coastal molder can lower both tooling cost and risk.

cities.name: Yongkang (Jinhua)
cities.summary: The 'hardware capital' — power tools, electric scooters, vacuum flasks and cups, doors, and a huge hardware trading market. Part of the Jinhua prefecture alongside Wuyi (fitness equipment) and Yiwu nearby.

markets.name: China Science & Technology Hardware City
markets.note: Vast hardware market plus surrounding factories; the default for tools and metal goods.

markets.name: Wuyi
markets.note: Nearby town that makes a large share of the world's gym and home fitness equipment.

cities.sourcing: For tools, e-mobility, and metal household goods, Yongkang's combined market-plus-factory model lets you compare quickly and then visit the maker. Pair with Wuyi for fitness gear.

cities.name: Haining (Jiaxing)
cities.summary: Home of China Leather City and a major warp-knitted-fabric and home-textile base. The default for leather garments, jackets, and leather goods at scale.

markets.name: China Leather City
markets.note: The country's flagship leather market with a deep surrounding production chain.

markets.name: Xucun
markets.note: Curtain and home-textile cluster.

cities.sourcing: For leather apparel and goods, Haining offers both a comparison market and the factories behind it; verify hide sourcing and finishing for European chemical-compliance (REACH) requirements.

cities.name: Huzhou
cities.summary: Zhili town is China's children's-clothing capital — a whole-town garment economy for kids' wear — alongside textiles and growing battery/new-energy tenants.

markets.name: Zhili
markets.note: One of the largest children's-wear clusters in the world; design, cut-make-trim, and wholesale in one town.

cities.sourcing: For kids' apparel programs, Zhili gives unmatched specialization and speed; mind child-product safety standards (EN 71, drawstring/small-parts rules) for export.

cities.name: Suzhou
cities.summary: One of China's strongest advanced-manufacturing cities: semiconductors, electronics, biotech, and nanotech, with the highest industrial output in Jiangsu. Kunshan, under Suzhou, is a global laptop and electronics base; Suzhou Industrial Park is a model high-tech zone.

markets.name: Kunshan
markets.note: Major notebook and electronics manufacturing center with deep Taiwanese-invested supply chains (Foxconn and others).

markets.name: Suzhou Industrial Park (SIP)
markets.note: Flagship hi-tech zone with strong IC, nano, and biomedical clusters and ready international-standard compliance.

cities.sourcing: For higher-tech and compliance-sensitive production, Suzhou factories are typically well above the national average on quality systems and certifications. A strong, lower-friction alternative to Shenzhen for some electronics.

cities.name: Wuxi
cities.summary: A quiet advanced-manufacturing powerhouse with one of the highest per-capita GDPs in China. A leading hub for semiconductor packaging and testing, photovoltaics, IoT, and biomedical devices.

markets.name: Wuxi Hi-Tech Zone
markets.note: Strong OSAT (packaging/test) base and a leading IoT cluster; home to major semiconductor and PV firms.

cities.sourcing: For solar products, IoT hardware, or high-precision medical devices, Wuxi factories tend to be high-quality with strong international-standard compliance — a good fit for European buyers.

cities.name: Changzhou
cities.summary: A solid machinery and new-materials city — photovoltaics, graphene research and application, general machinery, and a notable educational/teaching-equipment cluster.

markets.name: Wujin / Jintan zones
markets.note: Industrial parks with machinery, solar, and battery tenants.

cities.sourcing: A capable mid-tier option for machinery and PV components when you want YRD quality without first-tier-city pricing.

cities.name: Nantong
cities.summary: The global capital of home textiles — bedding, towels, curtains, and household linens — centered on the Diezhuang/Tongzhou market complex, plus shipbuilding and marine equipment.

markets.name: Diezhuang / Tongzhou Home Textile City
markets.hanzi: 叠石桥 / 通州家纺城
markets.note: The largest home-textiles trading and production cluster in the world; full chain from fabric to finished bedding.

cities.sourcing: For any bedding or household-linen program, Nantong is the source. Fabric weight, thread count, and finishing claims should be verified by test report, not catalog.

cities.name: Yangzhou
cities.summary: A specialist soft-toy and daily-brush city: a major plush-toy cluster, and Hangji town — the toothbrush and hotel-amenity capital that makes a huge share of the world's brushes.

markets.name: Hangji
markets.note: Makes a dominant share of the world's toothbrushes and hotel amenities; complete chain from bristle to packaging.

markets.name: Plush-toy cluster
markets.note: Large stuffed-toy and plush base.

cities.sourcing: For plush toys, mind EN 71 / small-parts safety. For oral-care and amenities, Hangji is the obvious source with deep cost competitiveness.

cities.name: Nanjing
cities.summary: A research-heavy provincial capital with electronics, petrochemicals, automotive, and a large software sector — strong on engineering talent rather than commodity manufacturing.

markets.name: Jiangbei New Area
markets.note: Designated IC and life-science development zone.

cities.sourcing: Better as an engineering and R&D base than a bargain-manufacturing city; useful when design capability matters more than unit cost.

cities.name: Xuzhou
cities.summary: China's construction-machinery heartland — home of XCMG, one of the world's largest heavy-equipment makers — with a deep supply chain for hydraulics, structural steel, and components.

markets.name: XCMG industrial base
markets.note: Anchors a full construction-equipment supply chain.

cities.sourcing: For construction and earth-moving equipment or their components, Xuzhou offers the deepest cluster; freight and after-sales support planning are the main challenges.

cities.name: Qingdao
cities.summary: Home of appliance giants Haier and Hisense, a strong home-textile and rubber/tire base, and — surprisingly — the false-eyelash capital of the world in its county-city Pingdu.

markets.name: Pingdu
markets.note: Makes the large majority of the world's false eyelashes — a cottage industry that became a global cluster.

markets.name: Haier / Hisense bases
markets.note: Anchor a deep northern appliance supply chain.

cities.sourcing: For beauty/lash products, Pingdu offers unmatched variety; many makers now hold vegan and cruelty-free certifications for Western markets. For appliances, Qingdao is the northern counterpart to Foshan's Shunde.

cities.name: Tianjin
cities.summary: The northern port-industrial center: aerospace, heavy machinery, petrochemicals, and a famous bicycle and e-bike cluster in Wuqing district.

markets.name: Wuqing
markets.note: One of China's largest bicycle and electric-bike clusters — frames, parts, and assembly.

markets.name: Binhai New Area
markets.note: Aviation, equipment, and petrochemical tenants.

cities.sourcing: For bicycles and e-mobility, the Wuqing/Tianjin cluster is a primary base alongside the Zhejiang towns; the northern port is efficient for heavy and bulk cargo.

cities.name: Weihai
cities.summary: A coastal city that quietly became the world's fishing-tackle capital — carbon-fiber rods and reels — plus tires and outdoor gear.

markets.name: Fishing-tackle cluster
markets.note: Makes a dominant share of the world's fishing rods; deep carbon-fiber composite know-how.

markets.name: Triangle Tire base
markets.note: Major tire manufacturing.

cities.sourcing: For fishing tackle or carbon-fiber outdoor products, Weihai is the specialist source with material expertise you will not find elsewhere at the same depth.

cities.name: Weifang
cities.summary: The world's kite capital and an agricultural-machinery and engine center (Weichai Power), with strong vehicle and components clusters.

markets.name: Kite cluster
markets.note: Hosts the international kite festival; makes a large share of the world's kites.

markets.name: Weichai / machinery base
markets.note: Heavy-duty engines and agricultural equipment.

cities.sourcing: Niche but unbeatable for kites and seasonal outdoor goods; a serious option for engines and farm machinery.

cities.name: Linyi
cities.summary: The wholesale-trade hub of northern China — sometimes called the northern Yiwu — with enormous commodity markets and a logistics network that reaches across the country.

markets.name: Linyi Commercial City
markets.note: Vast trading-market complex; the northern counterpart to Yiwu, strong on building materials and hardware.

cities.sourcing: For buyers serving northern China or Russia/Central Asia overland, Linyi's markets and logistics can beat Yiwu on freight; product mix skews to building materials and hardware.

cities.name: Heze (Caoxian)
cities.summary: Caoxian county turned e-commerce specialization into fame: it makes a huge share of China's hanfu and stage/performance costumes, plus woodcraft and (famously) export coffins.

markets.name: Caoxian
markets.note: Built an e-commerce-native garment economy around hanfu and costumes; also a major woodcraft and coffin exporter.

cities.sourcing: For traditional Chinese dress, dance, or cosplay-adjacent costume programs, Caoxian's e-commerce-native suppliers are fast and inexpensive.

cities.name: Xuchang
cities.summary: The global capital of human-hair and synthetic hair products — wigs, extensions, and hairpieces — anchored by Rebecca and a dense surrounding chain. Nearby Juancheng (Heze) is a complementary hair town.

markets.name: Hair-products cluster
markets.note: Makes the dominant share of the world's wigs and extensions; complete chain from raw hair processing to finished units.

cities.sourcing: For any hair-products line, Xuchang (with Juancheng) is the source. Confirm raw-material origin and labeling claims (human vs synthetic, sourcing) for Western retail compliance.

cities.name: Zhengzhou
cities.summary: A central-China logistics and electronics-assembly giant — home to Foxconn's largest iPhone campus — and a major rail and air-freight hub (a key China-Europe rail node).

markets.name: Foxconn 'iPhone City'
markets.note: One of the largest electronics-assembly campuses in the world.

markets.name: Air & rail freight hub
markets.note: Major China-Europe rail node and cross-border e-commerce gateway.

cities.sourcing: Strong for large-scale assembly and for inland logistics into Europe by rail; less of a small-batch sourcing destination than the coastal hubs.

cities.name: Wuhan
cities.summary: Central China's tech core, known as 'Optics Valley' for optoelectronics and optical fiber, and home to YMTC, China's leading 3D NAND flash maker. Strong in NEV (Dongfeng), biotech, and lasers, with deep university talent.

markets.name: Optics Valley (East Lake)
markets.note: The world's largest optical-fiber base and YMTC's NAND fab; lasers and displays too.

markets.name: Dongfeng NEV base
markets.note: Major automotive and NEV cluster.

cities.sourcing: For storage chips, optical components, and lasers, Wuhan is the top domestic destination; the central location makes it a strong logistics base for distributing across China.

cities.name: Changsha
cities.summary: The engineering-machinery capital of China — home of Sany and Zoomlion, two of the world's largest construction-equipment makers — plus a strong media and culture economy.

markets.name: Sany / Zoomlion bases
markets.note: Anchor a complete heavy-equipment supply chain — hydraulics, structural fabrication, controls.

cities.sourcing: The deepest cluster for concrete and construction machinery and their components; for OEM components, the tier-2 suppliers around Sany/Zoomlion are the place to look.

cities.name: Zhuzhou
cities.summary: China's rail-transit equipment center (CRRC) and a leading base for cemented carbide / hard-alloy cutting tools — the tungsten-carbide know-how behind a lot of precision machining.

markets.name: CRRC Zhuzhou base
markets.note: Locomotives, EMUs, traction systems.

markets.name: Hard-alloy cluster
markets.note: Cemented-carbide cutting tools and tungsten materials.

cities.sourcing: For carbide cutting tools and tungsten products, Zhuzhou has rare material depth; for rail systems it is the national center.

cities.name: Liuyang (Changsha)
cities.summary: The fireworks capital of the world — Liuyang and neighboring Liling/Wanzai make the dominant global share of fireworks and pyrotechnics.

markets.name: Fireworks cluster
markets.note: Centuries-old fireworks industry; the global center of production.

cities.sourcing: Specialist and heavily regulated — fireworks are dangerous goods with strict shipping, import-licensing, and safety requirements; work only with export-licensed makers and dangerous-goods forwarders.

cities.name: Hefei
cities.summary: Called 'China's best venture-capital city' for its state-investment-led model. Now a top hub for memory chips (CXMT), displays (BOE's largest base), new-energy vehicles (NIO, Volkswagen-Anhui), and home appliances.

markets.name: Xinzhan / IC zone
markets.note: CXMT (DRAM) and BOE's largest display fab anchor a mature electronics chain.

markets.name: NEV cluster
markets.note: NIO headquarters and Volkswagen-Anhui; a fast-growing EV supply base.

cities.sourcing: For advanced memory, displays, and EV components, Hefei's government-backed chains are very mature and well-equipped — a genuine new frontier for high-tech sourcing.

cities.name: Wuhu
cities.summary: An automotive and robotics city anchored by Chery, with growing industrial-robot, appliance, and new-materials clusters.

markets.name: Chery base
markets.note: Anchors a vehicle and components chain.

markets.name: Robotics cluster
markets.note: Growing robot and automation tenants.

cities.sourcing: A capable, lower-cost inland option for automotive components and automation hardware.

cities.name: Quanzhou
cities.summary: The birthplace of China's domestic sports brands (Anta, Xtep, 361°, Peak) and the heart of its athletic footwear and apparel industry. Its towns also lead in hygiene products, casual wear, stone, and ceramics.

markets.name: Jinjiang
markets.note: The engine of the sports-shoe industry and home of Hengan (diapers, tissues). Performance materials, lasts, and full private-label development.

markets.name: Shishi
markets.note: Large casual-apparel cluster with its own markets.

markets.name: Nan'an - Shuitou
markets.note: A national stone-trading and building-materials base.

cities.sourcing: For athletic footwear and sportswear private label, Jinjiang is the first stop — the factories understand performance materials and brand programs. For hygiene products, Hengan's ecosystem is deep.

cities.name: Jinjiang
cities.summary: A county-city of Quanzhou and the true heartland of the sports-shoe industry, plus China's hygiene-products giant Hengan. Listed separately because of its global weight.

markets.name: Chendai / Anhai shoe belt
markets.note: Dense shoe-factory and material chain; the place for performance-shoe OEM/ODM.

cities.sourcing: For technical athletic footwear, Jinjiang offers the deepest material and tooling chain in China; expect higher MOQs than fashion-shoe towns.

cities.name: Putian
cities.summary: A footwear-OEM city with a reputation for high-quality shoe construction (and, historically, replicas), now pushing into its own brands. Also a gold-jewelry and wood-carving center.

markets.name: Shoe-OEM cluster
markets.note: Strong shoe-making skill base; legitimate factories now run own-brand and white-label programs.

markets.name: Jewelry & woodcarving
markets.note: Notable gold-processing and religious-woodcarving clusters.

cities.sourcing: Putian's shoe-making craft is real; vet partners for IP integrity and insist on original designs and proper licensing to stay on the right side of brand and customs rules.

cities.name: Xiamen
cities.summary: A cross-strait gateway and a leader in third-generation (SiC/GaN) semiconductors and green tech. Home to San'an Optoelectronics, a major CATL battery base, and a dense cluster of Taiwanese-invested manufacturers.

markets.name: Torch Hi-Tech Zone
markets.note: San'an Optoelectronics anchors a third-gen semiconductor and LED cluster.

markets.name: CATL Xiamen base
markets.note: Advanced battery production.

cities.sourcing: For SiC/GaN devices used in EV fast-charging and 5G, Xiamen's third-gen cluster is the most advanced in China; the port is convenient for ASEAN shipping.

cities.name: Ningde
cities.summary: The global capital of EV-battery manufacturing — home of CATL, the world's largest battery maker — with a complete chain from raw materials to finished packs and energy-storage systems.

markets.name: CATL home base
markets.note: The entire battery supply chain is concentrated here, from cathode materials to finished packs.

cities.sourcing: For EV batteries, storage systems, or battery components, Ningde is effectively the only address you need — the chain density gives unmatched cost and quality, and suppliers are experienced with global EV brands.

cities.name: Dehua (Quanzhou)
cities.summary: A historic porcelain town famous for fine white porcelain (blanc de Chine) and now a dominant producer of ceramic arts, crafts, and decorative tableware for export.

markets.name: Ceramics cluster
markets.note: Deep tradition in fine white porcelain; strong on figurines, decor, and gift ceramics.

cities.sourcing: For decorative and craft ceramics, Dehua offers artistry plus export volume; plan carefully for the fragility and weight of the freight.

cities.name: Chongqing
cities.summary: A western megacity that became a laptop-manufacturing giant (a large share of the world's notebooks), a historic motorcycle base, and now a fast-growing NEV center (Changan, Seres/AITO with Huawei).

markets.name: Xiyong / laptop base
markets.note: Quanta, Inventec, and brand programs assemble a very large share of the world's laptops here.

markets.name: NEV cluster
markets.note: Changan and Seres/AITO anchor a growing EV and components chain.

cities.sourcing: For laptop-class electronics and NEV components at inland cost, Chongqing is strong; China-Europe rail from the region gives a freight edge to EU buyers.

cities.name: Chengdu
cities.summary: A western electronics and software hub — Foxconn iPad assembly, IC packaging, and a large games/software sector — with strong talent and improving China-Europe rail logistics.

markets.name: Hi-Tech Zone (West)
markets.note: Foxconn, IC packaging, and a deep software/games cluster.

cities.sourcing: Good for electronics assembly and software/firmware partners inland; rail to Europe is a logistics plus.

cities.name: Xi'an
cities.summary: Western China's hard-tech capital — aerospace, semiconductors (Samsung's largest overseas NAND fab), and solar (Longi, the world's largest solar maker) — and the starting point of the China-Europe Railway.

markets.name: Hi-Tech Zone
markets.note: Samsung NAND fab and Longi's solar base anchor mature chip and PV chains; labor costs below the coast.

markets.name: Aerospace base
markets.note: A leading aerospace-manufacturing center for China's programs.

cities.sourcing: Ideal for European buyers: the China-Europe Railway from Xi'an reaches the EU in roughly two to three weeks, faster than sea. Solar and semiconductor chains are mature, with lower inland labor costs.

cities.name: Baotou
cities.summary: The rare-earth capital of the world — the Bayan Obo deposit makes Baotou the center of rare-earth mining and processing — plus a large iron-and-steel base.

markets.name: Rare-earth zone
markets.note: Mining, separation, and magnet-material production at a scale found nowhere else.

cities.sourcing: For rare-earth materials and permanent magnets, Baotou is the upstream source; export of certain rare-earth items is subject to Chinese control regulations, so check current licensing rules.

towns.note: Global center of decorative and LED lighting; complete chain from drivers to fixtures.
One of the world's true toy capitals — plastic toys, RC, educational toys.
Billions of pairs a year; a whole-town sock economy.
Makes the majority of China's neckties and a huge share of the world's.
The 'button capital' — extraordinary concentration of buttons and trims.
National center for breakers, contactors, and industrial controls.
A dominant share of the world's toothbrushes and hotel amenities.
One of the largest kids'-wear clusters anywhere.
The global hub for freshwater-pearl farming, grading, and trade.
The world center of wire mesh and woven-wire products.
A vast luggage and bag manufacturing and trading town.
China's cashmere capital — raw and processed cashmere trade.
Makes a large share of the world's gym and home fitness gear.
One of the world's largest bra and underwear clusters.
E-commerce-native cluster for traditional dress, costumes, and woodcraft.
Makes the majority of the world's false eyelashes.
Dalang knitwear — one of the largest sweater towns on earth.

industry_index.products: Decorative, commercial, and LED lighting; LED chips & displays
Womenswear, casual wear, knitwear, kids' clothing
Woven & knit fabric, leather, home textiles, dyeing
Plastic toys, plush, RC, holiday & party goods
Power tools, hand tools, locks, kitchenware
Solar cells & panels, wind & storage equipment

ports.best: Heavy goods, auto parts, NEV exports (Ro-Ro)

shipping.best: Large orders, heavy or bulk cargo
Smaller orders, less than container load

incoterms.name: Ex Works
incoterms.who: Buyer handles everything from the factory door
incoterms.use: Maximum control; you arrange all transport and export.

incoterms.name: Free On Board
incoterms.who: Seller delivers to port; buyer handles shipping
incoterms.use: The default for most buyers — control over freight and carrier choice.

incoterms.name: Cost, Insurance, Freight
incoterms.use: Convenient for smaller orders when you don't want to manage freight.

incoterms.name: Delivered Duty Paid
incoterms.who: Seller delivers to your door, duties included
incoterms.use: Easiest for cross-border e-commerce and small buyers; watch for hidden margin.

finding: Identify the right city (and town) for your category before you shortlist suppliers.
Use B2B platforms (Alibaba, Made-in-China, Global Sources) and 1688 for domestic depth.
Visit the category trade show and the relevant wholesale market in person.
Get referrals; a warm introduction outperforms a cold inquiry.
Use remote factory tours and live-stream sourcing to pre-screen before traveling.
resilience: Diversify: keep a qualified backup supplier in a second town or region.
Map the full chain — your supplier's suppliers are where hidden bottlenecks live.
Hold 2-4 weeks of safety stock on critical components.
For some lines, pair China sourcing with regional/near-shore assembly to balance cost and risk.

qc.actions: Approve samples, lock specs in writing, agree AQL standards, confirm certifications and ESG.
Run in-line and mid-production inspections; monitor the timeline; resolve issues early.
Final random inspection, check packaging and drop-test, verify quantities and documents.

esg: Look for ISO 14001 (environmental) and SA8000 (social) certifications.
Ask for recent third-party audit reports, not just certificates.
Factories in advanced hubs (Suzhou, Wuxi, parts of Dongguan) more often have these ready.
For the EU, verify product-level compliance early: CE/UKCA, RoHS, REACH, GPSR, and category rules (EN 71 for toys, RED for radio).
negotiation: Always get 3-5 quotes minimum and compare on landed cost, not unit price.
Understand the true cost structure, including raw-material and tooling costs.
Negotiate on total value — payment terms, MOQ, lead time, QC — not just price.
Build long-term relationships; loyal customers get better terms and priority.
Be willing to walk away, and put every spec and date in writing.

glossary.def: Free On Board — seller delivers to the port of departure; buyer arranges and pays for the sea freight onward.
Minimum Order Quantity — the smallest run a supplier will accept, often negotiable on repeat business.
OEM builds to your design; ODM offers an existing design you can rebrand and tweak.
Acceptable Quality Limit — the sampling standard used in pre-shipment inspections to accept or reject a lot.
EMS = electronics contract manufacturing (box build). OSAT = outsourced chip packaging and test.
A shared public reference design / 'public board' — a ready-made hardware base that suppliers iterate on, especially in Shenzhen.
EU General Product Safety Regulation — broad safety and traceability obligations for products sold in the EU.
